mainEntity.name: How much does an M62 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M62 4.4 V8 typically costs £4,000–£6,000 supply only, with used units from £1,800–£3,200. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M62 4.4 V8 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M62 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). While the engine is smooth, powerful, and fundamentally well-built, the timing chain guides — made of brittle plastic — can fail catastrophically at around 100,000–150,000 miles. Regular coolant system maintenance is also essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M62 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide failure is the most critical — the plastic guides become brittle with age and mileage, disintegrate, and allow the chain to slip or break, causing catastrophic valve/piston interference. Cooling system leaks, valley pan gasket failures, and oil leaks are also common.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the M62?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Land Rover application, the M62 4.4 V8 powered the Range Rover P38A from 1994 to 2002. The engine was also used across BMW's 5 Series (E39), 7 Series (E38), and X5 (E53).

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover M62 the same as BMW's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M62B44 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover P38A applications used a slightly detuned version (280 hp) compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.

items.question: How much does an M62 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M62 4.4 V8 typically costs £4,000–£6,000 supply only, with used units from £1,800–£3,200. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

items.question: Is the BMW M62 4.4 V8 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M62 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). While the engine is smooth, powerful, and fundamentally well‑built, the timing chain guides — made of brittle plastic — can fail catastrophically at around 100,000–150,000 miles. Regular coolant system maintenance is also essential.

items.question: What is the most common M62 failure?
items.answer: Timing chain guide failure is the most critical — the plastic guides become brittle with age and mileage, disintegrate, and allow the chain to slip or break, causing catastrophic valve/piston interference. Cooling system leaks, valley pan gasket failures, and oil leaks from the valve covers are also common.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the M62?
items.answer: In Land Rover application, the M62 4.4 V8 powered the Range Rover P38A from 1994 to 2002. The engine was also used across BMW's 5 Series (E39), 7 Series (E38), and X5 (E53).

items.question: Is the Land Rover M62 the same as BMW's version?
items.answer: Yes — the M62B44 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover P38A applications used a slightly detuned version (280 hp) compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.
